# UC-10.16.10 · Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Alert Volume by Detection Source

## Description

High and medium Defender for Endpoint alerts grouped by detection source and category expose noisy engines, mis-tuned custom indicators, and sudden shifts toward a single detection pipeline that may indicate an active campaign or ingestion drift.

## Value

Surfaces which Defender detection sources drive alert volume so SOC leads can tune automation, suppressions, and hunting priorities before queues overflow.

## Implementation

Install Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security and enable the Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Alerts modular input (or equivalent API collection). Send data to a dedicated security index. Confirm field extractions for `detectionSource`, `severity`, `category`, and `signature` match your add-on version; rename in SPL if your deployment uses alternate spellings. Schedule hourly for trending; alert on week-over-week spikes for a single detection source.

## SPL

```spl
index=* sourcetype="ms:defender:atp:alerts" earliest=-24h
| eval src=coalesce(detectionSource, DetectionSource, "unknown")
| eval sev=lower(coalesce(severity, Severity, ""))
| where sev IN ("high","medium")
| stats count by src, category, signature
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Stacked bar chart (alerts by detection source), treemap (category × source), timechart of total high/medium alerts.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6207)
